title: Microlicia linophylla
intro: Microlicia linophylla is a plant species in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as a flowering plant. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Microlicia linophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Microlicia linophylla is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and cough.
taxonomy: Microlicia linophylla is classified as a species of plant within the family Melastomataceae. This family consists of approximately 170 genera and over 5,000 species, commonly known as the melastome or memecyclid families. The Melastomataceae family is part of the larger order Myrtales, which includes various flowering plants. The order Myrtales is a clade within the broader clade eurosids II, also known as the rosid clade. Microlicia linophylla's classification further emphasizes its relationships within the angiosperm clade.
